item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ha, that's more or less the reaction I was hoping to get. Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's actually a fair take, and if I ever decide to go back and add on to this I might make that change.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I liked the story - loved its concise and effective method - I disagree that the Head Researcher of a project would, by default, by the one in most proximity to it. Especially with a potentially memetic or cognito-hazardous "Compulsion Object" like this.

I think either:
a) It should be noted, and considered slightly odd, that Dr. Martinez was in such constant close-proximity to SCP-3565 (perhaps due to morbid fascination, perhaps it is another of SCP-3565's effects)
OR
b) It should be a lower level researcher, or perhaps a dedicated sanitary/custodial worker (janitor, "feeder", etc). This requires less explanation as to why they were in close proximity. Even if staff are rotated out, someone *has* to be the last on to be rotated; this person will have the longest "gestation" time - the others can have similar abnormal births, but this staff member can have the Martinez Experience.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So the idea that I was trying to convey is the way it messes with your biology is just a byproduct of being around it too much, hence why the head researcher, who I figured would be up close and personal with it more than anyone else assigned to it, was farther along than any of the other staff. Those effects are unrelated to any of its other anomalous properties.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So in the addendum, what causes the personnel to be that way? Has the meat been unfed? I might be the only person not to get it, but I'm not satisfied with the ending yet.

What I appreciate is while the description is brief, it's effective enough. Novote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was worried for a sec that this was just gonna be a meatier version of SCP-174, but I was pleasantly (?) surprised to see things go in a different direction. Concise, freaky, and tells a story without a lot of fluff. Good stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is my first ever SCP, so thanks for taking the time to read it! I'd love to hear what you think.

The image is from Pixabay: https://pixabay.com/en/meat-minced-close-up-raw-cooking-89107/
